subject: Human Resources
title: Workers Compensation
description: A fast breakdown of Workers Compensation for builders.

hook.text: A warehouse worker injures their back lifting heavy boxes. Workers' comp pays medical bills and lost wages—no lawsuit needed.

buildup.text: Workers' compensation is a no-fault insurance system—employees get benefits for work injuries regardless of who caused them.

discovery.text: In exchange, employees give up the right to sue their employer for negligence—a 'grand bargain' dating to the early 1900s.

twist.text: Fraud exists on both sides—employees faking injuries and employers misclassifying workers to avoid coverage.

climax.text: Effective return-to-work programs help injured employees recover and reintegrate—reducing costs and preserving careers.

punchline.text: Workers' comp trades the courtroom for the clinic. Both sides benefit.

quiz.question: What do employees give up in exchange for workers' compensation benefits?
options: The right to sue their employer for negligence
